steps.title: Isolate the fraction containing n
steps.content:
Start by moving $-\frac{2}{t}$ to the right side:
$$\frac{12}{n} = -\frac{2}{w} + \frac{2}{t}$$
This groups the terms we can combine.

steps.title: Combine the right side over a common denominator
steps.content:
The right side has denominators $w$ and $t$, so use $tw$ as the common denominator:
$$-\frac{2}{w} + \frac{2}{t} = \frac{-2t}{tw} + \frac{2w}{tw} = \frac{2w - 2t}{tw} = \frac{2(w-t)}{tw}$$
Now we have: $$\frac{12}{n} = \frac{2(w-t)}{tw}$$

steps.title: Invert both sides and simplify
steps.content:
When $\frac{12}{n} = \frac{2(w-t)}{tw}$, take reciprocals of both sides:
$$\frac{n}{12} = \frac{tw}{2(w-t)}$$
Multiply both sides by 12:
$$n = \frac{12tw}{2(w-t)} = \frac{6tw}{w-t}$$
That is the correct answer.
